title: The Stone Chorus Valley

features.name: the-singing-shelf
features.desc-on-touch: you run your palm across a shelf of stone voices, each chord still humming in the rock, the archive keeping what individual singers let escape

features.name: the-convergence-throat
features.desc-on-touch: you look into a geological throat where separate voice-streams meet and solidify into one stone utterance; the chorus has become landscape

features.name: the-fossil-song
features.desc-on-touch: you trace a fossilized spiral of sound frozen mid-vibration; it was singing when the stone caught it

features.name: the-separated-echoes
features.desc-on-touch: you pass between two great stone pillars, each holding one voice that will never merge with the other; they call across the valley forever

features.name: the-becoming-stone
features.desc-on-touch: you touch a surface in transition—half voice, half mineral—where individuality stops choosing and begins crystallizing

inscription: here at the stone chorus valley the many voices learn that silence is their final harmony and that petrification is a kind of preserving

file.name: Convergent_Voices_Field_Recording_Compressed.mp3
file.note: a live recording of five local singers in unison, but the file plays all five tracks simultaneously until they phase into a single inexplicable tone, then back out

exits.label: back toward the solitary chorus, where one voice begins to remember it was many
north where the valley narrows and the stone voices grow too close to distinguish
east toward a ridge where the echoes begin to argue with each other
